faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: The ten things first-time users ask. Each answer links to the page that handles it.

firstPet.q: Which species should a first-time keeper pick?
firstPet.a: Pick one with forgiving temperature/humidity needs and gentle handling. Leopard gecko, crested gecko, corn snake and bearded dragon are typical. Run the 8-question match quiz for a personalized top-3.

wildlifeLaw.q: Who has to file under the wildlife law?
wildlifeLaw.a: Effective 2025-12-14, owners of mammals, birds, reptiles and amphibians must file with the Ministry of Environment when keeping, transferring or losing the animal. White-list trades also require filings.

insurance.q: Can reptiles or amphibians get pet insurance?
insurance.a: Korean pet insurance is dog/cat only. Reptiles, amphibians and arthropods almost never qualify. Pettography recommends building an emergency reserve instead.

morphCost.a: Genetic rarity, combos (multiple morphs), and het/homozygous all matter. Korean baselines: common morphs ₩30–80k, mid ₩100–300k, premium ₩500k–1.5M.

enclosureSetup.q: How much does first-month setup cost?
enclosureSetup.a: Depends on category and tier. Reptile basic setup runs ~₩200–400k; premium ~₩600k–1M. The setup estimator gives a per-part breakdown.

feeding.q: Where do I buy feeder food, and how do I store it?
feeding.a: Crickets, dubia roaches, mealworms, superworms, pinky mice, CGD all differ by species. Order from KR shops (The Zoo, Newrun, Mimine Aqua, etc.); keep live insects in ventilated boxes and order only enough for 3–5 days.

emergency.q: What do I do in an emergency?
emergency.a: Each species has its own warning signs (refusal to eat, bad shed, trauma, heat shock). The /sos page gives a 1-minute category checklist and a 1-tap call to the nearest 24h exotic vet.

multiPet.a: The current build assumes one pet at a time. Multi-pet is on the roadmap; in the meantime, use /backup to keep separate JSON snapshots per animal.

dataLoss.q: Will I lose my data if I clear the browser?
dataLoss.a: Yes — everything is stored only in this browser's localStorage. Export regularly from /backup so you can restore on another device or after a browser wipe.

mapKey.a: The map renders only when VITE_KAKAO_MAP_KEY is set in your build environment. Self-hosters can grab a JavaScript key from the Kakao Developers Console.

about.title: One rare pet's whole life in one app
about.lead: Pettography curates every stage of a reptile/arthropod/exotic bird/amphibian/small mammal keeper's journey, from first pick to farewell, in a single Korean-context app.
about.whyBody: In a Korean pet market built around dogs and cats, owners of rare pets juggle scattered information. Major apps focus on dog/cat. Wildlife filing duties live buried on environment ministry sites. Pettography aims to be both the first tool you open in an emergency and the lightweight diary you check 2-3x a week.

location.title: Location and distance are first-class
location.body: All location data ships with distance, and switching location is one chip away.

emergency.title: Emergencies get priority
emergency.body: 24h vets, critical supply levels and environment range breaches lead visually and in sort order.

sources.title: Don't hide sources
sources.body: Mock data, external links, government registration status are all surfaced. Trust comes from metadata, not raw data.

diaryVsMedical.title: Light on diary, deep on medical
diaryVsMedical.body: Diary and routine are fast-entry; medical, habitat and budget pages carry rich context per screen.

beyondCards.title: Beyond identical cards
beyondCards.body: We avoid endless grids of the same card. Information weight drives the affordance choice.

data.storage: All user records (diary, health, habitat, budget, supplies, gallery, pet ID, ...) live only in this browser's localStorage.
data.backup: Use /backup to export everything as JSON and restore it on another device.
data.species: Species data is summarized from external references (Reptiles Magazine, MorphMarket, Korea National Biological Resources, etc.).
data.registry: Wildlife filing guidance summarizes Ministry of Environment material (wildlife.go.kr, animal.go.kr). Official sources remain authoritative.
data.external: 32 external links are real Korean sites verified with WebSearch + WebFetch; no affiliation.

limits.map: KakaoMap SDK not wired (placeholder). Set VITE_KAKAO_MAP_KEY to enable.
limits.whiteList: White-list classification is an estimate; refresh when MoE publishes the official list.
limits.multiPet: Multi-pet (one user, many animals) is not yet supported.
limits.insurance: Insurance comparison is dog/cat only. Exotic owners should rely on the reserve workflow.

contact.emptyHint: Feature requests, bug reports, general questions — all welcome.
contact.altHint: A topic-shaped channel may be faster — forum for keeper Q&A, consult for medical, partners for shop intake.

food.title: Feeder food guide
food.subtitle: 10 common feeder foods used by KR reptile/amphibian/arthropod owners with unit prices, nutrition notes and warnings. Filter by category.
food.disclaimer: Prices are 2025–26 KR averages. Nutrition values summarize supplier and research data.

genetics.title: Morph Genetics Calculator
genetics.subtitle: Pick the morph genotype of both parents and we compute the offspring phenotype odds with a Punnett square. Loci are assumed to be independent.
genetics.polygenicNote: Many popular morphs in this species are line-bred / polygenic and aren't captured by simple Mendelian math. Only single-gene morphs are modelled here.

results.title: Offspring odds
results.hint: Select at least one morph on a parent above to see the odds table.

save.title: Save pairing
save.label: Pairing name
save.empty: No saved pairings yet. Save a planned cross to keep it grouped per active pet.
save.needTrait: Select at least one morph on a parent first.

legend.title: Genetics quick reference
legend.body: A het (heterozygous) animal carries one recessive copy: it looks normal but passes the trait on. Co-dominant genes show a base morph with one copy and a distinct 'super' form with two. Dominant traits show with a single copy. A '66% het' tag is the chance a normal-looking sibling is a carrier.

molt.title: Shed / Molt Tracker
molt.subtitle: Log each shed and we'll predict the next one.

prediction.title: Next {{term}} prediction
prediction.needMore: Log at least 2 {{term}} events and we'll predict the next one. Add your first entry below.

overdue.title: {{term}} is overdue
overdue.generic: Past the expected cycle. Check humidity and hides, and if you see signs of a stuck shed, watch closely rather than forcing it off.
overdue.arthropod: Past the expected cycle. Pre-molt fasting and hiding are normal. Never disturb a molting specimen — just keep the humidity up.

tip.arthropodBody: Refusing food and hiding (or lying on its back) before a molt is normal. A molting or freshly molted specimen has a very soft exoskeleton — stop feeding and never handle it.

form.title: Log a {{term}}
form.notesPlaceholder: Note color changes, fasting, humidity, and other observations.

empty.title: No {{term}} records yet
empty.desc: Add your first entry and we'll analyze the cycle and predict the next one.

errors.notesMax: Notes must be 300 characters or fewer.

vivarium.title: Bioactive Vivarium Designer
vivarium.subtitle: Compose substrate layers, a cleanup crew, and plants into a self-cleaning enclosure.
vivarium.intro: A bioactive enclosure is a small ecosystem: a cleanup crew of isopods and springtails plus live plants break down waste and mould on top of a drainage, soil, and leaf-litter base. Dial in the right temperature and humidity gradient and routine maintenance drops dramatically.

diagram.title: Cross-section
diagram.empty: Pick substrate layers to draw the cross-section.
diagram.caption: Layers stack bottom to top.

maintenance.title: Maintenance estimate
maintenance.scoreLabel: Self-sustaining score {{score}} / 100 — higher means less work.

saved.title: Saved builds
saved.emptyDesc: Compose an enclosure above and save it to collect it here.

aquatic.title: This species is fully aquatic
aquatic.body: It needs an aquarium-based setup rather than a terrestrial bioactive vivarium. Cool temperatures, filtration, and the nitrogen cycle are what matter here — land substrate layers don't apply.

errors.emptyBuild: Select at least one substrate, crew, or plant.

morphs.title: Popular morph catalog
morphs.subtitle: Common morphs (color and pattern variants) sold in Korea with 2025–26 average price bands. Pick a base species to see morph cards and breeder sites.
morphs.disclaimer: Bands are 2025–26 market estimates. Actual prices vary by baby/adult, het/homozygous, and morph combinations.

setup.title: Enclosure setup estimator
setup.subtitle: Pick a category and we'll show the 6–8 essentials (enclosure, substrate, UVB, heat, hygrometer, food, etc.) with basic and premium price ranges. Standardizes what KR beginners most often forget.
setup.emptyDesc: Choose the category you want to keep from the chips above.
setup.totalHint: Averaged from KR e-commerce. Real prices vary by model and season.
setup.disclaimer: We have no affiliation with these shops. Prices and stock are governed by each shop's own listings.

insurance.title: Pet insurance & emergency reserve
insurance.subtitle: Korean pet insurance is dog/cat-only. Owners of reptiles, amphibians and arthropods almost never qualify; we recommend building an emergency reserve instead.
insurance.exoticWarningTitle: Heads up — exotic pets rarely qualify
insurance.exoticWarningBody: Your current category is excluded from most Korean pet insurance products. 100% of emergency vet bills are out of pocket, so building a reserve below is strongly recommended.
insurance.reserveDesc: Set aside a fixed amount each month for unexpected vet bills. Start date and total are stored in this browser.
insurance.reserveSuggestion: Target ~₩{{amount}} (3× {{name}}'s recommended monthly budget) to cover 1–2 emergency visits.

errors.contribution: Contribution must be a non-negative number.

insurance.providersDesc: Tap a card to open the official site. Actual premiums depend on breed, age and add-ons.
insurance.disclaimer: Premiums and coverage are governed by the insurer's official terms. This page is a curated guide.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.subtitle: Vaccinations, restock dates and wildlife filings due in the next {{horizon}} days, in one timeline.
calendar.emptyDesc: Add health/supply/registry items and they'll appear here automatically.

routine.title: Daily routine
routine.subtitle: Category-default tasks (feeding, misting, UVB, etc.) plus your own, grouped by daily/weekly/monthly cadence. A check stays 'done' only within the cadence window.
routine.noCategory: Pick a category in onboarding to auto-populate built-in tasks.
routine.empty: No tasks at this cadence yet.

backup.title: Backup & restore
backup.subtitle: All records (diary, health, habitat, budget, supplies, gallery, pet ID, etc.) live only in this browser. Export regularly so you can continue on another device or browser.
backup.exportDesc: Download all current data as a single JSON file.
backup.importDesc: Pick a previously exported JSON file to overwrite existing data and reload.
backup.importedToast: Restored. The page will reload shortly.
backup.invalidFileToast: This is not a valid Pettography backup file
backup.wipeDesc: Erase every record and start from a clean slate. Cannot be undone.
backup.wipeConfirm: Erase everything? This cannot be undone.

petid.title: Pet ID card
petid.subtitle: Printable card for the inside of an enclosure or your wallet, in case your pet escapes. Includes owner contact and finder instructions. All data stays in this browser.
petid.previewHint: When you print, only the card on the right is output (other UI hidden).
petid.cardFooter: If you found my pet, please contact the owner above. 발견 시 위 연락처로 연락 부탁드립니다.
petid.requiredHint: Fill pet name, species, owner name and phone to enable printing.

placeholders.distinctMarks: e.g. partial tail loss, white spot on back
placeholders.foundInstructions: e.g. Nocturnal, prefers darkness. Avoid direct sunlight; place in a paper box and contact me.

compare.title: Compare species
compare.subtitle: Pick up to {{max}} species and compare difficulty, lifespan, space, handling and monthly cost in one table. Share the URL.
compare.empty: Pick at least one species to compare.

registry.title: Wildlife filing guide (KR)
registry.subtitle: Regulated wildlife (mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians) require Ministry of Environment filings when kept, transferred, or deceased. Track your own filing status here.
registry.effectiveNote: Effective: 14 Dec 2025 (Wildlife Protection & Management Act, KR)
registry.noCategory: Pick a category in onboarding first

keeping.title: Keeping filing
keeping.desc: Register with the Ministry of Environment wildlife system when you first begin keeping a regulated animal.

transfer.title: Transfer filing
transfer.desc: Both parties file when an animal is sold or transferred; the receiver attaches enclosure photos.

death.title: Death filing
death.desc: Report the death and disposition method (burial, cremation, agency) when your wildlife pet dies.

microchip.title: Microchip (optional)
microchip.desc: Not legally required, but logging species and chip ID after RFID implantation improves recovery if the animal is lost.

registry.disclaimer: Ministry of Environment guidance is the final authority. This is a user-facing summary, not legal advice.
